vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1369

plan.title: swarm: Improve reliability and tooling for long-running merge/review cascade automation in Grok Build hybrid path

narratives.Description: swarm: Improve reliability and tooling for long-running merge/review cascade automation in Grok Build hybrid path. The current cascade pattern relies on hand-authored monitors that babysit each PR; a first-class task surface that survives the documented harness fragility removes the manual-babysitting failure mode.
narratives.Overview:
## Context

During the 1166 strategy consistency swarm, we reached the merge phase with all five PRs at 5/5.

We attempted to use an automated background monitor (following the "auto-merge when ready" pattern) to:
- Wait for #1363 to become mergeable after its rebase.
- Automatically squash-merge it with admin rights.
- Then immediately merge the three Wave 3 PRs.

This approach had to be abandoned. The monitor ran for a long time reporting the PR as blocked (with no usable head data), even though direct inspection showed the state was genuinely blocked due to checks not having completed on the new head after the force-push.

We eventually killed the monitor and performed the remaining work manually.

## Problem

The current tooling and patterns for reliable long-running automation in the Grok Build hybrid swarm path are insufficient:

- `pr_merge_readiness.py` (and similar tools) are fragile in the harness (see #1366).
- There are no first-class, resilient primitives for "wait until this PR is mergeable, then merge it".
- Visibility into background `spawn_subagent` processes is very limited (#1365).
- Long-running monitors have no good way to stay healthy when individual data sources become temporarily unreliable.

This makes automated merge cascades (a natural part of swarm work) painful and unreliable.

## Impact

- Forces monitors to either babysit manually or risk the automation getting stuck for long periods.
- Increases cognitive load and error surface during the critical merge phase of a swarm.
- Makes the hybrid Grok Build swarm path feel less mature for real, multi-PR work.

## Proposed Improvements

1. **Resilient PR state monitoring helper**
   - Create a more robust utility (e.g. in `scripts/`) for polling PR mergeability / review state that gracefully handles transient failures from `pr_merge_readiness.py` and falls back to direct REST parsing of the Greptile comment + check runs.
   - Expose a simple "is this PR currently mergeable on its latest head?" function with clear diagnostics.

2. **First-class cascade automation support in the swarm skill**
   - Add recommended patterns / small helpers for "merge cascade" automation.
   - Consider a `task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge` style surface (or equivalent in Python) that is resilient in the Grok Build environment.

3. **Better visibility & signaling for sub-agents**
   - Improve the contract and tooling around long-running `spawn_subagent` processes so monitors can more reliably detect whether agents are alive, making progress, or stuck (building on #1365).

4. **Documentation & patterns**
   - Add clear guidance in the swarm skill and AGENTS.md for how to safely build and run long-running merge/review automation in the hybrid path, including known limitations and workarounds.

5. **Platform feedback (Grok Build side)**
   - The underlying harness fragility around subprocess output capture on Windows (recurring `UnicodeDecodeError` in reader threads, see #1353 and #1366) makes this class of tooling inherently brittle. This should be raised as platform feedback.

## References

- This specific occurrence: 1166 swarm merge cascade monitoring for #1363 + Wave 3.
- #1365 (sub-agents going dark / visibility)
- #1366 (harness fragility breaking monitoring tools)
- #1368 (detailed root cause of this exact monitor failure)
- #1353 (original Windows harness capture issues)

This feels like an important area to strengthen if we want the hybrid Grok Build swarm path to support serious, multi-wave review + merge workflows reliably.
narratives.Cluster: Wave 3 (cascade automation, depends on #1366 Wave 1 + #1365/#1368 Wave 2) of the Grok Build harness fragility cluster. Cross-ref #1353 (original Windows capture limitation). Cluster tracked via the `cluster:grok-harness` tag across four vBRIEFs (no GitHub umbrella issue filed).
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm monitor coordinating a merge cascade on Grok Build, I want a first-class task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge surface that survives the harness's known fragility, so that I can automate multi-PR cascades without babysitting individual checks.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Add scripts/pr_wait_mergeable.py that takes a PR number, polls mergeability via the resilient scripts/monitor_pr.py from #1368, and merges with gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --admin only when the readiness call exits CLEAN on current HEAD. Three-state exit (0 merged / 1 timeout or escalation / 2 config error).
2. Add a tasks/pr.yml target pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge that wraps the script with bare {{.CLI_ARGS}} forwarding and chains task pr:check-protected-issues -- <N> (Layer 3 #701) before any merge call.
3. Update skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 1 (Merge) and Phase 6 Step 5 (Slack Announcement) to cite the new task as the canonical cascade automation surface; preserve the existing 'monitor proposes order, user approves' gate by requiring explicit per-PR approval before the helper fires.
4. Add an AGENTS.md rule documenting that cascade automation on Grok Build hybrid path MUST use task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge and reaffirm the per-PR atomic gate (task pr:merge-ready && gh pr merge).
5. Add tests/cli/test_pr_wait_mergeable.py covering PR-becomes-ready-then-merges (exit 0), timeout (exit 1), protected-issue-link-present (exit 1 before merge call), and config error (exit 2).
6. Add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry referencing #1369 and the #1366, #1368 dependencies.
narratives.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1369 and the #1166 swarm cascade-automation failure on Wave-3 PRs. Depends on #1366 (safe-subprocess helper) and #1368 (resilient monitor).

narrative.Acceptance: When given a PR number, the helper polls mergeability through the resilient monitor from #1368, calls gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --admin only after the readiness call exits CLEAN on current HEAD, and emits a three-state exit (0 merged / 1 timeout / 2 config error) with structured stdout.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1369 -- scripts/pr_wait_mergeable.py resilient cascade helper.

narrative.Acceptance: When invoked via the new target, it forwards CLI_ARGS via the established bare-{{.CLI_ARGS}} convention, chains the Layer-3 protected-issue check from #701 before any merge call, and surfaces the script's exit code unchanged.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1369 -- task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge Taskfile surface.

items.title: Swarm skill documents the new cascade automation surface

narrative.Acceptance: When the swarm skill Phase 6 Step 1 or Step 5 surfaces, it shows task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge as the canonical cascade automation entry point and preserves the existing per-PR explicit-approval gate that rejects unapproved merges.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1369 -- Swarm skill documents the new cascade automation surface.

items.title: AGENTS.md mandates the new surface for cascade automation

narrative.Acceptance: When an agent reads AGENTS.md, it shows a project rule citing #1369 that rejects cascade automation on the Grok Build hybrid path which does not go through task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge, and the rule reaffirms the per-PR atomic gate.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1369 -- AGENTS.md mandates the new surface for cascade automation.

items.title: Tests cover ready, timeout, protected-link, and config-error paths

narrative.Acceptance: When pytest runs tests/cli/test_pr_wait_mergeable.py, it validates the four paths and asserts that the merge call only fires after the underlying readiness call returns CLEAN and after the Layer-3 protected-issue check returns empty; otherwise the helper rejects the merge and emits the documented exit code.
narrative.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1369 -- Tests cover ready, timeout, protected-link, and config-error paths.

references.title: Issue #1369: swarm: Improve reliability and tooling for long-running merge/review cascade automation in Grok Build hybrid path

expected_outputs: task pr:wait-mergeable-and-merge merges a CLEAN PR and timeouts cleanly on an unready PR
swarm SKILL.md Phase 6 cites the new task
AGENTS.md carries a rule mandating the new task for Grok Build cascade automation
